title: Pogonia bifaria
taxonomy: Pogonia bifaria is classified in the family Orchidaceae and the genus Pogonia. The genus Pogonia is a part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its unique orchid flowers. The species Pogonia bifaria is native to North America, specifically in the eastern and central regions. It is a terrestrial orchid, which grows in woodland environments.

intro: Pogonia bifaria, also known as Islay orchid or two-flowered grass pink, is a rare and endangered plant species native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Bioactive constituents of Pogonia bifaria include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
